title: MCP server support
spec: Give Helm real MCP tools. Create workspace/mcp/servers.json defining free, no-auth MCP servers runnable via npx: a filesystem server rooted at /Users/owner/secondme, and a fetch/web server. Change index.js and imessage.js so the ask() Claude args load this file via --mcp-config workspace/mcp/servers.json (keep --strict-mcp-config). Make it degrade gracefully if a server fails to start (Helm must still reply). Document usage in workspace/CLAUDE.md. Add a smoke check (next free number in workspace/tests/smoke.mjs): assert workspace/mcp/servers.json is valid JSON with at least one server, and that index.js references it. Do not require network at test time.
_build:
Everything is in order. Here is a concise summary of what was built:

---

**MCP server support — what was added**

**`workspace/mcp/servers.json`** (new file)

Defines two free, no-auth MCP servers launched via `npx -y`:
- `filesystem` — `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem`, rooted at `/Users/owner/secondme`. Gives Claude read/write file tools inside the repo without raw shell calls.
- `fetch` — `@modelcontextprotocol/server-fetch`. Gives Claude an HTTP fetch tool for reading web pages or APIs.

**`index.js` and `imessage.js`** (modified)

Both files gain a `mcpConfigArg()` helper that:
1. Tries to parse `workspace/mcp/servers.json` (absolute path resolved at startup).
2. Returns the file path on success, so Claude Code loads it with `--mcp-config`.
3. Falls back to the inline `{"mcpServers":{}}` string if the file is missing or malformed, so Helm always starts and replies even when MCP servers can't be loaded.

The `--strict-mcp-config` flag is retained, keeping the user's global MCP config isolated from Helm's session.

**`workspace/CLAUDE.md`** (updated)

New "MCP servers" section explains both servers, their capabilities, the graceful-degradation behavior, and how to add/remove servers by editing `servers.json`.

**`workspace/tests/smoke.mjs`** (check 18 added)

Asserts, without any network calls:
1. `workspace/mcp/servers.json` exists and is valid JSON.
2. `mcpServers` has at least one entry.
3. `index.js` contains the string `workspace/mcp/servers.json`.

**To use:** start either bot normally (`node index.js` or `node imessage.js`). On the first message, Claude Code will launch the MCP servers via `npx` (downloading packages on first run, cached afterwards). Claude can then use `mcp__filesystem__*` and `mcp__fetch__*` tools in addition to its built-in tools.

title: Web browsing via Playwright
spec: Add browser automation as registry tools. npm install playwright (and run its chromium download lazily/once). Create workspace/tools/impl/browser.open.mjs, browser.read.mjs, browser.click.mjs, browser.fill.mjs, browser.screenshot.mjs driving a persistent context with profile dir workspace/browser-profile so logins persist. Register all in workspace/tools/registry.json. Add a smoke check that `node workspace/tools/tools.mjs list` includes browser.open and that the modules import without launching a browser (lazy import). Keep the heavy chromium download out of the smoke path.

title: True local embeddings for recall
spec: Upgrade memory recall with real semantic embeddings, no paid API. Use @xenova/transformers (npm) with all-MiniLM-L6-v2 running locally. Add workspace/memory/embed.mjs that computes/caches a vector per fact (store in a vectors table in memory.db keyed by fact id). Modify memory.mjs recall to blend embedding cosine similarity with the existing keyword/TF-IDF score; fall back to current behavior if the model isn't downloaded yet or on --keyword-only. Keep recall's JSON output shape identical. Add a smoke check that recall still returns the existing the seeded example facts and the same shape (model download must NOT be required for smoke to pass — guard it).
_build:
All green. Here is a concise summary of what was built:

---

## What was added

**`workspace/memory/embed.mjs`** — new module (never imported at the top level of anything, always lazy)

Exports five functions:
- `ensureVectorsTable(db)` — creates a `vectors` table in `memory.db` (`fact_id PK, vector TEXT, model, created`)
- `isModelAvailable()` — attempts to load `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` with `allowRemoteModels=false`; returns `true` if the weights are cached locally, `false` otherwise, never throws
- `embedText(text)` — runs the pipeline and returns a 384-dim normalised float array
- `cosineSimilarity(a, b)` — pure math helper, handles null/length-mismatch gracefully
- `getOrComputeVector(db, factId, text)` — reads from `vectors` cache or computes and stores it

**`workspace/memory/memory.mjs`** — recall case upgraded

The switch block is now wrapped in `await (async () => { ... })()` so the recall case can be async while every other verb stays unchanged. Inside recall, after the existing TF-IDF block, there is a `try/catch` block that:
1. Dynamically imports `embed.mjs`
2. Calls `isModelAvailable()` — if false, skips silently
3. If true, computes the query embedding and retrieves/computes per-fact vectors via `getOrComputeVector`
4. Blends with `Math.max(k, e * 0.9)` instead of `Math.max(k, s * 0.9)`

When the model is absent, behavior is bit-for-bit identical to before. Output JSON shape is unchanged.

**`workspace/tests/smoke.mjs`** — test 18 added

Checks: all five functions exported, `cosineSimilarity` correct on unit vectors, `isModelAvailable` returns a boolean without throwing, `recall --keyword-only` still returns the seeded example facts. Model download is not required.

**`package.json`** — `@xenova/transformers: ^2.17.2` added.

**To activate real embeddings:** download the model once (one-time, ~22 MB):

```js
const { pipeline } = await import('@xenova/transformers');
await pipeline('feature-extraction', 'Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2');
```

After that, every `recall` call uses actual sentence embeddings and caches vectors in `memory.db`.

title: Multi-step planning subsystem
spec: Add workspace/plans/ with plan.mjs (uses node:sqlite, plans.db). A plan = {id, goal, created, status} with steps {id, plan_id, idx, task, tool_or_cmd, status, checkpoint, result}. CLI verbs: create <goal>, add-step <plan_id> <task>, next <plan_id> (returns next pending step), complete <plan_id> <step_id> [--result], show <plan_id>, list. The scheduler and swarm can resume a plan by querying next. Output JSON. Add a smoke check covering create -> add-step -> next -> complete -> show round-trip. Document in CLAUDE.md.

title: Vision: understand the screen
spec: Add workspace/tools/impl/vision.mjs: take a screenshot (screencapture -x to a temp png), then use Claude's own image reading (spawn `claude -p` with the image path and a question) to describe the screen or locate a UI element and return its click point. Account for the Retina scale (divide pixel coords by the display scale, default 2) so the returned points work with bin/guicontrol. Verbs: describe, find <query> (returns {x,y} in points). Register in workspace/tools/registry.json. Add a smoke check that the tool is registered and the module imports (do NOT require a real screenshot/permission at test time — guard it).
_build:
All good. Here is a summary of what was added:

---

**What was added**

`workspace/tools/impl/vision.mjs` — a new tool with two verbs:

- **`describe`** — runs `screencapture -x` to capture the screen silently to a temp PNG (default `/tmp/helm-vision.png`), then spawns `claude -p` in `bypassPermissions` mode with a prompt that instructs Claude to use its Read tool to read the image and return a concise description of what is on screen.

- **`find <query>`** — same screenshot step, then asks Claude to locate the named UI element and return only a JSON `{"x": N, "y": N}` with pixel coordinates. The coordinates are divided by `--scale` (default `2` for Retina displays) before output, so the returned `{x, y}` are in points, ready for `bin/guicontrol click X Y`.

Both verbs accept `--out <path>` to override the screenshot destination and `--scale <n>` to override the Retina divisor. The `main()` function is gated behind `process.argv[1] === import.meta.url`, so importing the module has no side effects.

**Registry** — two entries appended to `workspace/tools/registry.json`: `vision.describe` and `vision.find`, each pointing to the script with the appropriate verb pre-baked into `exec`.

**Smoke check 18** — verifies that both tool names appear in `tools.mjs list` output and that the module imports cleanly. It does not require Screen Recording permission or a real screenshot.

**Usage examples:**
```
node workspace/tools/impl/vision.mjs describe
node workspace/tools/impl/vision.mjs find "Save button"
node workspace/tools/tools.mjs call vision.find --json '{"query":"address bar"}'
```

title: Helm dashboard (local web UI)
spec: Create workspace/dashboard/server.mjs: a Node http server (node:http only, NO external deps) on http://localhost:7777 serving one self-contained HTML page (inline CSS/JS, auto-refresh every 10s) showing Helm's live state: launchd service status for com.helm.discord/think/scheduler/selfupgrade/dashboard (parse `launchctl list`), memory stats + recent facts/preferences (read memory.db via node:sqlite), scheduler jobs (jobs.db), latest think journal entries, recent self-upgrade history (tail UPGRADE_LOG.md), active fleet target, and last 5 git commits. Add a JSON endpoint /api/state. Create a launchd plist workspace/dashboard/com.helm.dashboard.plist (RunAtLoad, KeepAlive, logs to workspace/dashboard/dashboard.log) but do NOT attempt to load it from the build. Add a smoke check that server.mjs imports and that starting it briefly then GET / returns HTTP 200 (use an ephemeral port in the test). Keep it dependency-free and clean, dark, readable.
_build:
All 18 tests pass. Here's a summary of what was added:

---

**What was added**

`workspace/dashboard/server.mjs` — A dependency-free Node.js HTTP server (using only `node:http`, `node:sqlite`, `node:child_process`, `node:fs`) that serves two endpoints:

- `GET /` — A self-contained dark-themed HTML page that auto-refreshes every 10 seconds via `fetch('/api/state')`. Displays seven panels: launchd service status (with green/red indicators) for `com.helm.discord`, `com.helm.think`, `com.helm.scheduler`, `com.helm.selfupgrade`, and `com.helm.dashboard`; memory stats and the 12 most-recently-updated facts/preferences from `memory.db`; all scheduler jobs from `jobs.db` with next-run times; the 5 most-recent think journal entries as expandable `<details>` blocks; the last 5 self-upgrade log sections; the active fleet target (`workspace/active-target`); and the last 5 git commits.
- `GET /api/state` — Returns the same data as JSON.

The `start(port)` export takes a port (defaults to 7777) and returns `{ server, url }` for testing. When run directly it starts on port 7777; a `--port N` flag overrides this.

`workspace/dashboard/com.helm.dashboard.plist` — launchd plist with `RunAtLoad`, `KeepAlive`, logs to `workspace/dashboard/dashboard.log`. Not loaded automatically from this build — load manually with `launchctl load workspace/dashboard/com.helm.dashboard.plist`.

**Smoke check added** (test 18) — imports `server.mjs`, starts it on an ephemeral port (OS-assigned), checks `GET /` returns HTTP 200 with the expected title, checks `GET /api/state` returns valid JSON with all required keys, then closes the server.

**To use:** `node workspace/dashboard/server.mjs` then open `http://localhost:7777` in a browser.
